reforms.description: The Austrian government agreed upon a wide-ranging tax reform that includes a new top tax rate of 55% for income above EUR 1 million, aimed at financing research and education investment projects.
reforms.package_name: Tax Reform for Research and Education
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax reform is designed to increase funding for research and education, which are critical for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax reform has been enacted and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in the tax system aimed at enhancing public investment in research and education.
reforms.source_quote: In March 2015, the government agreed upon a wide-ranging tax reform that is expected to be legislated by mid-2015 and will enter into force on 1 January 2016.

reforms.description: Austria aims to implement an integrated policy framework to enhance gender equality, which includes making the tax and benefit system more employment-friendly, extending care service infrastructure, and encouraging flexible workplace practices.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The impact of the gender equality framework on economic growth is ambiguous as it may improve labor market participation but could also involve significant public expenditure.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy framework has been officially announced but not yet legislated.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant policy change aimed at addressing gender disparities in the labor market.
reforms.source_quote: An integrated policy framework to make Austria more gender-equal should build on 3 pillars: i) make the tax and benefit system more employment friendly; ii) extend the care service infrastructure; and iii) encourage more flexible workplace practices.
